function.name: get_tool_schema
function.description:
Get the JSON schema for one or more tools from a connected MCP server. Call this before invoke_tool to get the required parameters for any tool you need to use.

Available MCP servers and their tools:

## GitHub MCP
add_comment_to_pending_review, add_issue_comment, add_reply_to_pull_request_comment, assign_copilot_to_issue, create_branch, create_or_update_file, create_pull_request, create_pull_request_with_copilot, create_repository, delete_file, fork_repository, get_commit, get_copilot_job_status, get_file_contents, get_label, get_latest_release, get_me, get_release_by_tag, get_tag, get_team_members, get_teams, issue_read, issue_write, list_branches, list_commits, list_issue_types, list_issues, list_pull_requests, list_releases, list_tags, merge_pull_request, pull_request_read, pull_request_review_write, push_files, request_copilot_review, run_secret_scanning, search_code, search_issues, search_pull_requests, search_repositories, search_users, sub_issue_write, update_pull_request, update_pull_request_branch

## Notion MCP
create-comment, create-database, create-pages, create-view, duplicate-page, fetch, get-comments, get-teams, get-users, move-pages, search, update-data-source, update-page, update-view

## Pylance MCP
get_docstring, search_documentation, check_file_syntax_errors, analyze_imports, installed_top_level_modules, invoke_refactoring, python_environments, run_code_snippet, settings, check_syntax_errors, update_python_environment, workspace_roots, workspace_user_files

server.description: The name of the MCP server to query, e.g. 'github', 'notion', or 'pylance'.

tools.description: List of tool names to get the schema for (e.g. ['create_issue', 'create_pull_request']).

function.name: invoke_tool
function.description: Invoke a specific tool on a connected MCP server. First use get_tool_schema to retrieve the required parameters, then call this tool with the appropriate arguments.

server.description: The name of the MCP server that has the tool, e.g. 'github', 'notion', or 'pylance'.

tool.description: The name of the tool to invoke, e.g. 'create_issue'.

arguments.description: The arguments to pass to the tool, as defined by the schema returned from get_tool_schema.
